phenomenon: Aeolian dust lifting and atmospheric loading
key_measurement: Global mineral dust emission ~2,000 Tg/year; Saharan dust dominates at ~670 Tg/year; fluid threshold wind friction velocity for mineral dust ~0.20–0.25 m/s (Kok et al., 2021, Nature Reviews Earth & Environment)
institutions: NASA Goddard Institute for Space Studies (GISS)
University of California Los Angeles (UCLA) Department of Atmospheric and Oceanic Sciences

sources.citation: Kok, J.F., Storelvmo, T., Karydis, V.A., Adebiyi, A.A., Mahowald, N.M., Evan, A.T., He, C., Leung, D.M. (2023) 'Mineral dust aerosol impacts on global climate and the hydrologic cycle', Nature Reviews Earth & Environment 4:71–86

Prospero, J.M., Ginoux, P., Torres, O., Nicholson, S.E., Gill, T.E. (2002) 'Environmental characterization of global sources of atmospheric soil dust identified with the NIMBUS 7 Total Ozone Mapping Spectrometer (TOMS) absorbing aerosol product', Reviews of Geophysics 40(1):1002
NASA MERRA-2 — Modern-Era Retrospective Analysis for Research and Applications, Version 2, GMAO/NASA GSFC

visualization_compliance: Saltation threshold wind speed must be indicated (~5–8 m/s at 10 m height for fine sand); do not imply dust lifts at calm conditions
Dust plume transport trajectories must be consistent with observed MODIS/MISR imagery — Saharan dust to Amazon basin is documented, label with source
Aerosol optical depth (AOD) color scale must be anchored to real MODIS retrieval range (0–2+)
Particle size distribution must distinguish clay (<2 µm), silt (2–50 µm), and coarse dust (>50 µm) modes
Do not conflate smoke aerosol with mineral dust in composite visualizations without explicit labeling
Label MODELED/REANALYSIS on any global dust loading maps; cite MERRA-2 or equivalent dataset
